summary: All 3 critical bugs verified as FIXED. Bug 1: Legal Chat now returns real AI responses in French citing Belgian law (Claude API direct httpx call working). Bug 2: Findings display actual text using issue/details fallback chain. Bug 3: French UI labels working with simplified language aliases (fr=fr-BE).

Bug 1 - Legal Chat API.evidence: POST /api/chat/send returns real AI response in French citing Belgian law (Article 82 de la Loi du 3 juillet 1978)

Bug 1 - Multi-turn chat.evidence: Follow-up message with conversation_id remembers context about Belgian dismissal notice

Bug 1 - James UI.evidence: James avatar (dark blue J), 'Disponible maintenant' badge, credential bar with '847 000+', trust badges, French suggested questions for Belgian jurisdiction

Bug 1 - Chat input.evidence: Chat input works, sends messages, shows typing indicator, receives AI response

Bug 2 - Findings text.evidence: ai_findings display actual text using issue/details fallback: 'Vague breach allegations...', 'Unreasonably short 10-day response deadline...'

Bug 3 - French UI labels.evidence: CaseDetail shows 'Score de Risque Jasper', 'Analyse IA Jasper', 'Constatations cles', 'Televerser un nouveau document', 'Prochaines etapes recommandees'

Bug 3 - Language aliases.evidence: labels['fr'] = labels['fr-BE'] alias working - user.language='fr' maps to French translations

Bug 3 - Backend language injection.evidence: analyze_document_advanced accepts language parameter (line 781), get_language_instruction added to system prompt

Jurisdiction/Language selectors.evidence: Top bar shows jurisdiction button (Belgium) and language button (Francais)

context_for_next_testing_agent: All 3 bugs fixed and verified: (1) Legal Chat uses direct httpx call to Claude API instead of call_claude() which was returning parsed JSON, (2) Findings text uses fallback chain: text || texte || description || constatation || issue || details || finding, (3) CaseDetail labels have simplified aliases: labels['fr'] = labels['fr-BE']. The chat API takes 10-15 seconds for Claude response. User language is 'fr' (simplified from 'fr-BE' after migration).
